features.title: One Ghost, real decisions
features.body: Each commando is a valuable field asset. Support units can extend an operation, but the loss of the Ghost remains the critical failure point.

features.title: Hunt neural critters
features.body: Some jungle specimens flee when approached. Stun them first, then harvest before they recover and vanish into cover.

features.title: Builds stay personal
features.body: Commandos may specialize as marksmen, assault troops, demolitions experts, or field support.

features.title: Craft tactical constructs
features.body: Lights, walls, stations, turrets, companion tools, and Maelstrom Mines provide temporary battlefield control.

enemies.lore: The genetically versatile hive spawn every Zerg strain metamorphoses from. Psychically sensitive and still carrying the code for any breed, a single surviving larva can rebuild a whole colony.

evolutions.name: Spawnling
evolutions.change: Passive hive marker drifting near a nest. Harmless alone, but it flags an active breeding site worth clearing or avoiding.

evolutions.name: Clutchling
evolutions.change: Nests produce larvae faster and in tighter clusters, feeding local mob density if the site is left standing.

evolutions.name: Molter
evolutions.change: Larvae harden mid-metamorphosis, capable of a weak defensive lash if provoked before finishing their morph.

evolutions.name: Broodwell
evolutions.change: Dense larval clusters erupt into a wave of fresh spawn when disturbed, punishing anyone who clears a nest carelessly.

enemies.lore: Contains the genetic code to morph directly into any Zerg structure, and gathers the minerals and vespene gas that fuel the swarm's metabolism.

evolutions.name: Forager
evolutions.change: Low HP scout-worker. Mostly tests player aim and reveals where the swarm is spreading.

evolutions.name: Gnawer
evolutions.change: Gains more HP and a faster bite, making ignored worker packs dangerous.

evolutions.name: Stripper
evolutions.change: Gains bonus damage against structures and deconstruct targets, punishing static defenses.

evolutions.name: Ravager
evolutions.change: Fast raider with high structure damage and enough HP to slip through weak perimeter fire.

enemies.lore: Bred almost instantly from a slain host by a Queen's spawn broodling attack. Short-lived, but proof a real enemy just died to produce it.

evolutions.name: Hatchling
evolutions.change: Very fragile melee flood unit that appears in numbers.

evolutions.name: Skulker
evolutions.change: More movement speed and HP, better at surrounding isolated Ghosts.

evolutions.name: Frenzy
evolutions.change: Much faster attack cooldown; dangerous if allowed to stack on a player.

evolutions.name: Berserker
evolutions.change: Tiny berserker variant with high speed and burst damage, forcing quick repositioning.

enemies.lore: Mutated from absorbed dune-runner DNA into a fast, razor-clawed killer, spawned in pairs from a single larva and cheap enough to throw at anything.

evolutions.name: Skitter
evolutions.change: Basic fast melee enemy: low HP, low damage, short vision.

evolutions.name: Chaser
evolutions.change: Gains +HP and movement speed, becoming reliable chase pressure.

evolutions.name: Ripper
evolutions.change: Switches to a sharper claw profile: higher damage and shorter cooldown.

evolutions.name: Slasher
evolutions.change: Elite hunter with max speed, high damage, and enough durability to punish wounded players.

enemies.lore: Its upper carapace opens to expose hundreds of spines, projected with enough force to punch through armor plating, though it moves slowly across open ground.

evolutions.name: Spitter
evolutions.change: Short-range spine attack with modest damage.

evolutions.name: Piercer
evolutions.change: Gains HP and +1 weapon range, letting it fire from behind melee mobs.

evolutions.name: Needler
evolutions.change: Swaps to a faster needle weapon with better cooldown and higher damage.

evolutions.name: Lancer
evolutions.change: Long-range elite shooter with high damage, forcing players to break line of sight or snipe it first.

enemies.lore: Bred from the Hydralisk strain, this defensive warrior must burrow to fire subterranean spine waves that pierce every unit in a line, friend or foe.

evolutions.name: Burrower
evolutions.change: Slow setup ambusher with low damage spikes.

evolutions.name: Trapper
evolutions.change: More HP and stronger spine damage, holding narrow paths better.

evolutions.name: Impaler
evolutions.change: Longer spine range and higher splash pressure, threatening grouped players.

evolutions.name: Skewer
evolutions.change: Elite area-control unit with very long reach and lethal line damage if not cleared quickly.

enemies.lore: An airborne hive warden with no attack of its own, instead fouling enemies with ensnaring spores and spawning broodlings from slain hosts.

evolutions.name: Matron
evolutions.change: Light support threat with low HP and short-range harassment.

evolutions.name: Warden
evolutions.change: Gains HP and faster movement so it can stay behind the front line.

evolutions.name: Hexer
evolutions.change: Switches to a longer-range disruption weapon/effect to pressure priority targets.

evolutions.name: Oracle
evolutions.change: High-priority support unit with long range and punishing debuff-style pressure.

enemies.lore: The swarm's basic air-to-ground striker. Its glaive wurm bounces between targets, hitting hardest on the first strike and fading with each bounce.

evolutions.name: Wing
evolutions.change: Light air threat with low HP and short-range poke.

evolutions.name: Skirmisher
evolutions.change: Gains movement speed and HP, making it harder to kite.

evolutions.name: Razorwing
evolutions.change: Upgraded bouncing glaive damage for harassment against grouped players.

evolutions.name: Stormwing
evolutions.change: Fast elite flyer with higher damage and longer range, demanding anti-air weapon swaps.

enemies.lore: Believed to be the nesting form of the Mutalisk, trading speed and altitude for a thicker carapace and a longer-ranged plasma barrage.

evolutions.name: Siegewing
evolutions.change: Slow air artillery with low damage but long enough range to teach target priority.

evolutions.name: Bulwark
evolutions.change: Gains HP and stronger siege damage against structures.

evolutions.name: Shellwing
evolutions.change: Longer range and higher impact damage, forcing the squad to move or counter-snipe.

evolutions.name: Dreadwing
evolutions.change: Elite siege threat with very long range and heavy structure/player pressure.

enemies.lore: A biochemical cancer factory that feeds on other Zerg to fuel plague and dark swarm, and is nearly helpless without an escort.

evolutions.name: Bloater
evolutions.change: Slow fragile special unit with a small death warning.

evolutions.name: Gorger
evolutions.change: Gains HP and a larger death burst radius.

evolutions.name: Plaguebearer
evolutions.change: Death effect becomes plague-style damage over an area, punishing close-range kills.

evolutions.name: Pestilence
evolutions.change: Large plague/explosion death effect that can break grouped players if killed carelessly.

enemies.lore: A living tank with monomolecular mandibles that shear through armor plating and infantry alike, the intimidating crown of Zerg evolution.

evolutions.name: Crusher
evolutions.change: Slow tank with high HP but low early damage.

evolutions.name: Juggernaut
evolutions.change: Gains armor and a larger HP pool, requiring sustained fire.

evolutions.name: Mauler
evolutions.change: Gains speed and a stronger melee weapon, becoming a real chase threat.

evolutions.name: Behemoth
evolutions.change: Boss-like elite with heavy armor, huge HP, and punishing melee damage.

weapons.title: Railgun Carbine
weapons.body: Long-range single-target rifle. Steady auto-fire keeps pressure on one enemy at a time from a safe distance. Craft with minerals and gas to unlock; once crafted, it can be freely re-equipped later.
weapons.special: An extended-range charged shot that deals bonus damage to a single elite target.

weapons.title: Riot Incinerator
weapons.body: Close-range cone weapon. Short auto-fire reach demands the Ghost hold ground, but it clears everything in front. Craft with minerals and gas to unlock; once crafted, it can be freely re-equipped later.
weapons.special: Ignites a cone in front of the Ghost, burning every enemy caught inside over a few seconds.

weapons.title: Arc Autocannon
weapons.body: Bouncing-shot cannon. Auto-fire arcs between nearby enemies instead of committing to one target. Craft with minerals and gas to unlock; once crafted, it can be freely re-equipped later.
weapons.special: Fires a chaining bolt that jumps to additional nearby targets before dissipating.

weapons.title: Corrosive Sprayer
weapons.body: Damage-over-time sprayer. Auto-fire applies a light corrosive coat rather than big upfront damage. Craft with minerals and gas to unlock; once crafted, it can be freely re-equipped later.
weapons.special: Coats a target in corrosive residue that continues dealing damage over several seconds.

weapons.title: Storm Rifle
weapons.body: Burst-fire rifle. Auto-fire unloads in rapid clusters instead of a steady stream, favoring short trades. Craft with minerals and gas to unlock; once crafted, it can be freely re-equipped later.
weapons.special: Unloads a rapid three-round burst for a short window of high damage per second.

weapons.title: Demolition Launcher
weapons.body: Explosive splash weapon. Auto-fire is slower and awkward at close range but punishes grouped enemies. Craft with minerals and gas to unlock; once crafted, it can be freely re-equipped later.
weapons.special: Lobs a delayed-fuse warhead that detonates in a blast radius around the impact point.

weapons.title: Twin-Spike Driver
weapons.body: Piercing-line weapon. Auto-fire rounds punch through the first target to strike whatever stands behind it. Craft with minerals and gas to unlock; once crafted, it can be freely re-equipped later.
weapons.special: Fires a paired piercing round that passes through the lead target to hit a second enemy in line.

weapons.title: Lockdown Snare Gun
weapons.body: Slow and control weapon. Auto-fire is weak, trading damage for reliable crowd disruption. Craft with minerals and gas to unlock; once crafted, it can be freely re-equipped later.
weapons.special: Freezes a target in a stasis field, halting its movement and attacks for a few seconds.

structures.title: Maelstrom Mine
structures.body: Burrowed stun device armed at a fixed point. Detonates under the first enemy that crosses its trigger radius, interrupting rushes or setting up a critter harvest.

structures.title: Bunker
structures.body: Garrisons a wounded commando behind cover for safe supporting fire, trading mobility for a hardened firing position during a stand.

structures.title: Light Post (Psi Emitter)
structures.body: Gradually brightens the surrounding ground and keeps new mobs from spawning nearby. The anchor of any safe pocket.

structures.title: Palisade Wall
structures.body: Cheap barrier that delays and redirects incoming mobs, buying time to reposition or finish a harvest.

structures.title: Regeneration Station
structures.body: Stationary recovery point that speeds HP return for any commando standing nearby.

structures.title: Turret
structures.body: Automated defensive fire for a temporary hold. Strong alone, stronger as part of a covered perimeter.

structures.title: Signal Flare Beacon
structures.body: Deployable flare that marks a rally point and briefly scares off newly spawned mobs, buying a window to regroup.

upgrades.title: Field systems: max health
upgrades.body: Generic mineral-and-gas upgrade that raises Ghost vitality so a commando can survive surrounds, night collapses, and failed withdrawals long enough to recover or be rescued.

upgrades.title: Field systems: armor and shields
upgrades.body: Generic mineral-and-gas upgrade for armor plating, shield capacity, or regeneration layers. Reduces sudden deaths without replacing good positioning.

upgrades.title: Field systems: mobility and vision
upgrades.body: Generic mineral-and-gas upgrade for patrol speed, scouting range, kiting, and withdrawal options so the squad can choose fights instead of being pinned.

upgrades.title: Field systems: harvest efficiency
upgrades.body: Generic mineral-and-gas upgrade that reduces exposure while gathering from trees, rocks, crystals, organic growth, and other resource points.

upgrades.title: Field systems: construct utility
upgrades.body: Generic mineral-and-gas upgrade for repair value, deconstruction refunds, alerts, deploy support, and temporary hold strength.

upgrades.title: Field systems: companion support
upgrades.body: Generic mineral-and-gas upgrade for support assets such as robotic helpers, controlled specimens, or resurrected organisms. Companions support the Ghost rather than replacing them.

gameplayLoop.title: Five-night operation
gameplayLoop.body: Hold the sector through five night cycles. Extraction conditions remain unconfirmed.

gameplayLoop.title: Daylight creates choices
gameplayLoop.body: Use safe hours to pick one priority: gather supplies, train weapons, fortify, or relocate.

gameplayLoop.title: Night punishes delay
gameplayLoop.body: When enemy movement increases, hesitation costs more than withdrawal.

gameplayLoop.title: Failure costs momentum
gameplayLoop.body: Death removes field assets and carried resources, but retained training lets the operation continue.

resources.title: Trees
resources.body: Common mineral-heavy supply nodes with minor gas yield.

resources.title: Bushes
resources.body: Fast organic harvest points suited to moving patrols.

resources.title: Rocks
resources.body: Mineral-rich deposits requiring a safer work window.

resources.title: Crystals
resources.body: Vespene-rich formations usually found deeper in hostile territory.

resources.title: Organic growth
resources.body: Balanced biological matter, often near contested ground.

resources.title: Sap vines
resources.body: Slow-drip vespene ooze tapped from thick canopy vines, safe to work under cover.

resources.title: Basalt veins
resources.body: Exposed volcanic mineral seams. Heavier yield, but a longer exposed harvest window.

resources.title: Spore pods
resources.body: Pressurized vespene pods that burst on contact. Fast yield, but risky near active mobs.

resources.title: Neural critters
resources.body: Mobile wildlife specimens. Stun or trap them before harvest.

notes.title: Reuse unused abilities as spells
notes.body: Review dormant StarCraft abilities and retain only effects that read clearly in combat.

notes.title: Command card stays readable
notes.body: Keep the command card operationally clear: one weapon special from the equipped weapon, one chosen active ability, and structures/utilities in their own lane.

notes.title: Few active hotbar abilities
notes.body: Weapon packages swap the weapon-specific target command. Separately, crafted active abilities compete for one equipped defensive/offensive active slot.

notes.title: Psi Emitter feasibility
notes.body: Test a deployable emitter that lures hostile movement or amplifies nearby field effects.

activeAbilities.title: Kinetic Barrier
activeAbilities.body: Defensive active ability. Craft with minerals and gas to unlock, then equip as the current active system to absorb or blunt a dangerous engage.

activeAbilities.title: Emergency Medburst
activeAbilities.body: Defensive active ability. Unlock once, then equip when the squad needs a clutch self-save or ally recovery button instead of extra offense.

activeAbilities.title: Overclock Sprint
activeAbilities.body: Defensive mobility active. Equip for rapid withdrawal, rescue runs, or reposition windows when night pressure closes in.

activeAbilities.title: Shock Pulse
activeAbilities.body: Offensive active ability. Equip to interrupt a rush, briefly stun clustered mobs, or create space around a harvest point.

activeAbilities.title: Orbital Spike
activeAbilities.body: Offensive active ability. Equip for a high-impact ground-target strike against dense waves or elite threats.

activeAbilities.title: Toxic Cloud
activeAbilities.body: Offensive active ability. Equip to contaminate a ground area, softening mobs that path through a choke or around a light pocket.
